attacking_style: Counterattacking in final meters of climbs after allowing breakaway riders to absorb pace-making duties. Tactical patience over early aggression, striking late when rivals are committed.
team_tactics: Sunweb employs energy-conservation strategy, allowing breakaways to control tempo before late rider intervention. Minimal domestique expenditure, maximizing leader freshness for decisive moments.
terrain_preference: Medium-length climbs where late accelerations can succeed (Qurayyat-type finishes). Benefits from situations where others do the work early.
vulnerability: Insufficient data across race types. Single observation suggests potential weakness in sustained high-tempo scenarios where he cannot rely on counterattack tactics.
key_patterns: Wins via tactical patience rather than sustained dominance or early attacks
Counterattacks in final meters after rivals exhaust themselves controlling pace
Team conserves energy by leveraging breakaway dynamics instead of controlling race
form_trajectory: positive: first professional victory at 22 demonstrates emerging stage race capabilities and tactical maturity
